content.text: Answer 4 questions. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote theorems (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text: Prove that the following polynomials are irreducible in \(\mathbb{Q}[x]\), stating clearly any theorems you wish to apply.
Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with \(1\ne 0\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an element \(r\in R\) to be nilpotent.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that the set of all nilpotent elements of \(R\) forms an ideal (called the nilradical of \(R\)).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Show that the nilradical is contained in every prime ideal of \(R\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that the function \(N(a+b\sqrt{-3})=a^2+3b^2\) is multiplicative, that is, \(N(rs)=N(r)N(s)\) for all \(r,s\in R\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \(R\) is not a unique factorization domain.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Is \(R\) a principal ideal domain? Justify your answer.

problem.text: Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with 1 and let \(M\) be a module over \(R\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State what it means for \(M\) to be a Noetherian \(R\)-module, and for \(R\) to be a Noetherian ring.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(M\) is a Noetherian \(R\)-module, then every nonempty set of submodules has a maximal element.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example of a non-Noetherian commutative ring with 1.

problem.text: Using the rational canonical form, find one representative of each conjugacy class of elements of order 3 in \(\mathrm{GL}(4,\mathbb{Q})\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an element of \(E\) to be algebraic over \(K\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(\alpha,\beta\in E\) are algebraic over \(K\), then \(K(\alpha,\beta)\) is finite-dimensional over \(K\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Deduce that the elements of \(E\) that are algebraic over \(K\) form a field.
